_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: LOCAL batch — Houston TX (Willie Davis) + San Antonio TX (Mungia D4, Meza Gonzalez D8, McKee-Rodriguez D2, DA Joe Gonzales). Evidence-based finalization, rosters web-verified June 2026. Scores reset to null where no evidence; TRUE/FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists. Roster corrections: Willie Davis WON the Dec-2023 At-Large-2 runoff (DB note 'lost' was wrong) and is the sitting member; Mungia/Meza Gonzalez/McKee-Rodriguez all won the 2025 SA elections and are sitting; Joe Gonzales won 2022 (not 2024) and announced June 2025 he is NOT seeking re-election (term ends 2026-12-31).

profile.religion: Baptist pastor — founder/senior pastor, MacGregor Palm Community Baptist Church (Houston, founded 2010); board member, Houston Christian University
profile.background: U.S. Army veteran; Houston native (Third Ward). Chaplain, Harris County Republican Party. Elected Houston City Council At-Large Position 2 in the Dec 9, 2023 runoff (defeated Nick Hellyar); sworn in Jan 2, 2024.

willie-davis.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] ROSTER CORRECTION: prior 'lost runoff' premise was WRONG — Willie R. Davis WON the Dec 9, 2023 runoff for Houston City Council At-Large Position 2 over Nick Hellyar and is the sitting member (houstontx.gov/council/2; Ballotpedia). Part of the 2023 GOP-endorsed conservative sweep of the at-large seats. HARD evidence: as a Baptist pastor he was a lead organizer of the 2014-15 campaign to REPEAL Houston's Equal Rights Ordinance (HERO), arguing it would let 'transgender, transsexual and gender expression' into restrooms/showers and 'open a door' to predators, and that 'the LGBT community' has no 'right to take away other people's rights' (Houstonia 8/2014; CW39 8/14/2014) — scored biblical_marriage q2 (rejects transgender ideology) and q4 (opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy) FALSE-for-rubric=TRUE-alignment. Affirmatively pro-police: endorsed by the Houston Police Officers' Union, calls for more officers and tighter sentences on violent offenders, launched the faith-based 'Police, Pastors & People' anti-crime initiative — meets the high bar for public_justice q0 (TRUE). NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, school choice/curriculum, taxes/budget votes, election administration, immigration, or guns at the council level — those cells null, not penalized. Marriage q0 (explicit one-man-one-woman affirmation) left null — no direct statement located despite his pastorate.

2.note: Lead organizer of the HERO-repeal drive; publicly argued the ordinance wrongly lets 'transgender, transsexual and gender expression' into restrooms/showers — rejects transgender ideology.

4.note: Organized the petition campaign to repeal Houston's LGBTQ non-discrimination ordinance (HERO); opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy: Houston Police Officers' Union endorsement, calls to hire more officers and impose tighter sentences on violent offenders, launched the 'Police, Pastors & People' anti-crime initiative (high-bar affirmative advocacy, not routine budget).

profile.background: Lifelong District 4 (SW-side) San Antonio resident; B.A. Political Science, Loyola University New Orleans (2015); ~9 years a District 4 council aide; former MOVE Texas board member; appointed to South San ISD board in 2018. Won SA City Council District 4 outright May 3, 2025 (56.78%, no runoff); sworn in June 18, 2025. San Antonio council is officially nonpartisan; he is Democratic-aligned (endorsed Gina Ortiz Jones for mayor).

edward-mungia.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] ROSTER: won SA City Council District 4 outright May 3, 2025 (56.78%, no runoff), sworn in June 18, 2025; succeeded Adriana Rocha Garcia (who lost the mayoral race). Stale 'lost/candidate' framing corrected to active incumbent. HARD evidence: spoke against ICE at a council meeting — 'ICE doesn't belong here,' called its tactics 'another ugly side of violence of this country's history,' and asked that local ICE operations be posted online for transparency (KENS5) — documented contrary to deportation enforcement (border q1) and to opposing sanctuary policy (border q2). On police he favors 'collaboration between law enforcement and the community' but in the FY2026 budget argued AGAINST simply adding 65 patrol officers (favoring specialized units) — community-policing posture that does NOT meet the high bar for affirmative pro-police advocacy, so public_justice q0 left null (neither clear backing nor a documented defund vote). NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, marriage/transgender (beyond an LGBTQ-inclusive housing-task-force chairmanship — too thin to score), school choice/curriculum, Christian liberty, specific tax/budget votes, election integrity, COVID/subsidiarity, E-Verify, or guns. Church affiliation not stated in any source.

1.note: Publicly opposed ICE enforcement — 'ICE doesn't belong here,' called its deportation tactics 'violence' — documented contrary to mandatory deportation.

2.note: Spoke against ICE and asked that local ICE operations be posted publicly; sanctuary-sympathetic posture, contrary to opposing sanctuary-city policy.

profile.background: San Antonio native; UTSA grad, St. Mary's University School of Law grad; former chief of staff to Mayor Ron Nirenberg (2018-2021); former SA River Authority. Won SA City Council District 8 in the June 7, 2025 runoff (57.35% over Paula McGee); sworn in June 18, 2025. Ran in the Democratic primary for Bexar County Judge in 2022. Council office is officially nonpartisan.

ivalis-meza-gonzalez.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] ROSTER: won SA City Council District 8 in the June 7, 2025 runoff (57.35%), sworn in June 18, 2025; still holds the seat. CONTEXT: arrested July 25, 2025 for DWI (second DWI; council suspended her committee assignments, pursued a censure, then reinstated her Jan 13, 2026); she did not resign. SPARSE DATA — only two documented rubric data points, so the headline ~50% reflects thin research, not a balanced record. HARD evidence: ran on full police FUNDING ('provide the full funding necessary to address and reduce crime,' SAFFE neighborhood officers) and was endorsed by the San Antonio Police Officers Association, the firefighters, and the Bexar County Deputy Sheriff's Association — affirmative LE support meeting the high bar (public_justice q0 TRUE). She also accepted the endorsement of the Stonewall Democrats (an LGBTQ Democratic group) per her campaign site — an endorsement-based signal of alignment with LGBTQ political aims (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; not her own words, flagged as endorsement-derived). NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, marriage definition/transgender (her own words), school choice/curriculum, Christian liberty, specific tax/budget votes, election integrity, COVID/subsidiarity, immigration/ICE, or guns. Church affiliation not stated.

0.note: Affirmatively pro-police: campaigned on 'the full funding necessary' for first responders and SAFFE officers; endorsed by the San Antonio Police Officers Association, firefighters, and the Bexar County Deputy Sheriff's Association (high-bar affirmative LE support).

4.note: Accepted the endorsement of the Stonewall Democrats (LGBTQ Democratic group) per her campaign endorsements page — endorsement-based evidence of alignment with promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy (not a personal statement).

profile.background: UTSA Communication B.A.; former math teacher at Sam Houston High School. First openly gay man on San Antonio City Council (elected June 2021); re-elected to a third term May 3, 2025 (61.37% vs 7 challengers). Endorsed by the LGBTQ+ Victory Fund. Council office is officially nonpartisan; he is a documented progressive Democrat.

jalen-mckee-rodriguez.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] ROSTER: re-elected to a third District 2 term May 3, 2025 (61.37%); sitting. Rich documented progressive record. HARD evidence: explicitly pushed San Antonio's Reproductive Justice Fund to pay for out-of-state ABORTION travel ('I explicitly asked for the fund to include travel for abortion') and voted with the 6-5 majority to add $100K to it (TPR 4/2024; KSAT 4/3/2025) — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE. Introduced the city's first Trans+ History Week proclamation and authored the 'LOVIN' trans-restroom/ID policy as an openly gay LGBTQ advocate (SA Report 5/2026) — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. On policing he proposed cutting $5.7M from SAPD's proposed increase and voted AGAINST the 2022 police-union contract citing weak civilian oversight, framing it as 're-imagination of public safety' (TPR 9/2021; KSAT 5/2022) — public_justice q0 FALSE. On ICE: 'We should not collaborate or cooperate with ICE,' filed a memo to limit cooperation and rallied opposition to an East Side ICE facility (SA Current; KSAT 2/2026) — border q1/q2 FALSE. refuse_state_overreach left null: his clashes with TX state preemption (SB4) were to enable sanctuary policy, the opposite valence of the rubric's intent — scoring it would mislead. NO EVIDENCE found on guns/2A, election integrity, school choice/curriculum, or Christian liberty. Church affiliation not stated.

0.note: Pushed the city's Reproductive Justice Fund to pay for out-of-state abortion travel ('I explicitly asked for the fund to include travel for abortion') — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted with the 6-5 majority (April 3, 2025) to add $100K to the city's abortion-travel Reproductive Justice Fund — actively funds abortion access, opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Openly gay LGBTQ-advocacy councilmember (LGBTQ+ Victory Fund candidate); does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

2.note: Authored the 'LOVIN' trans-affirming policy (restroom access, ID-accuracy affirmation) and introduced Trans+ History Week — affirms transgender ideology.

4.note: Introduced San Antonio's first Trans+ History Week proclamation and authored trans-rights city policy — promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: Proposed cutting $5.7M from SAPD's proposed budget increase and voted against the 2022 police-union contract citing weak oversight; advocates 're-imagination of public safety' — contrary to backing law enforcement.

1.note: 'We should not collaborate or cooperate with ICE'; rallied opposition to an ICE detention facility — contrary to mandatory deportation enforcement.

2.note: Filed a memo to limit city cooperation with ICE to 'what is strictly required' — sanctuary-aligned, contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

profile.background: Sitting Bexar County Criminal District Attorney (San Antonio). Born San Antonio; B.A. + J.D., St. Mary's University; former municipal judge/magistrate and criminal-defense attorney. First elected 2018 (defeated incumbent Nico LaHood), re-elected 2022. Announced June 2025 he will NOT seek re-election; serving out his term, which ends Dec 31, 2026 (open seat on the Nov 3, 2026 ballot).

joe-gonzales.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] ROSTER CORRECTION: prior brief mis-dated his re-election to 2024 — he won 2018 and 2022, and announced June 2025 he is NOT seeking re-election (candidacy_status not_running); he is a lame-duck DA whose term ends Dec 31, 2026 (open seat Nov 2026). Documented Soros/PAC-funded reform prosecutor. HARD evidence: Texas Justice & Public Safety PAC (Soros-funded) spent ~$958K backing his 2018 win and ~$570K in 2022, with Soros personally adding $1M+ in Oct 2022 (Fox News). Policies: stopped prosecuting <1 oz marijuana and trace drug amounts (misdemeanor pot cases fell from ~4,500 in 2018 to 16 in 2020, Reason 5/2019) and runs cite-and-release diversion (3,500+ diverted from jail by 2022, TPR) — public_justice q1 FALSE (rogue/soft-on-crime reform-prosecutor). His Civil Rights Division prosecuted SAPD officers and the police union (SAPOA) president publicly called on him to resign (KSAT 8/2023) — public_justice q0 FALSE (clashed with police, did not back them). One of 5 Texas DAs who signed the June 2022 joint prosecutors' letter and said he has 'no plan to prosecute' abortion seekers (News4SA) — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE. Backed the Bexar County immigration-defense fund and called ICE raids 'deplorable'/'outrageous,' a sanctuary-sympathetic posture (TPR 2/2026) — border q2 FALSE; border q1 left null (he also resisted prosecuting ICE agents — no clear deportation-policy statement). NO EVIDENCE found on marriage/LGBTQ, election-fraud prosecution, or guns; bail reform is only soft/inferred (no direct act located). Church affiliation not stated.

0.note: Signed the June 2022 joint prosecutors' letter and said he has 'no plan to prosecute' Texans seeking abortions — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pledged non-prosecution of abortion seekers post-Dobbs — opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

0.note: Created a Civil Rights Division that prosecuted SAPD officers; the police union (SAPOA) president publicly called on him to resign (Aug 2023) — did not back law enforcement.

1.note: Soros/Texas Justice & Public Safety PAC-funded reform prosecutor: stopped prosecuting <1oz marijuana/trace drugs and runs cite-and-release diversion — the soft-on-crime/Soros-DA profile the cell flags.

2.note: Backed the Bexar County immigration-defense fund and called ICE raids 'deplorable'/'outrageous' — sanctuary-sympathetic, contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.
